response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack engineer with genuine staff-level seniority and a broad systems background, but the role requires 8+ years building production AI solutions — a bar he does not meet. His GenAI exposure is limited to roughly one year at re:cinq (MCP tooling and agentic workflow infrastructure), and his resume contains no mention of Python, vector databases, RAG architectures, LLM evaluation pipelines, or major multi-agent frameworks such as LangGraph or CrewAI. While his MCP work is a direct match to one listed key technology, the absence of the core AI/ML stack makes him a poor fit for a Staff-level GenAI specialist role at this point in time.
strengths: Demonstrated staff-level seniority across multiple organizations with evidence of technical leadership, mentoring, and cross-functional impact
Direct, current experience building MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers and contributing to agentic workflow infrastructure — an explicit key technology in the JD
Strong distributed-systems and data-engineering background (high-throughput microservices at Dunnhumby, event-driven GIS platform, AWS CDK at Optilyz) that is transferable to AI infrastructure work
concerns: Python is entirely absent from his listed skills and work history, yet it is the primary language called out in the required qualifications
No evidence of RAG architectures, vector databases, LLM performance metrics, or multi-agent frameworks — all central to the role's technical requirements
GenAI experience totals approximately one year (current role only), far below the mandatory 8+ years of production AI solution development

key_factors.factor: Absent core AI stack (RAG, vectors, LLMs)
